response.justification: Bogdan has 15+ years of solid software development experience and genuine breadth across stacks, but this role is fundamentally a people-leadership position and there is no evidence of people management anywhere in the resume — no direct reports, no performance reviews, no org-level change leadership. Developer tooling exposure exists (CI setup, test frameworks, MCP generators) but is incidental rather than a sustained focus area, falling short of the 3-year minimum. The candidate appears well-suited for a senior or staff IC role, not a mid-to-senior engineering manager position.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Demonstrated developer tooling contributions including test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD setup, and MCP code generators
Strong knowledge-sharing culture evidenced by public workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects
concerns: No people management experience — no direct reports, no performance management, no hiring or mentorship responsibilities mentioned at any employer
Developer tooling depth is incidental and scattered rather than representing a sustained 3-year focus as required
No evidence of strategic roadmap ownership, cross-team alignment, or organizational change leadership
